steps.title: Set up and apply the discriminant condition
steps.content:
For the equation $2x^2 - 4x = t$ to have no real solutions, rearrange to standard form $2x^2 - 4x - t = 0$ where $a = 2$, $b = -4$, $c = -t$. Apply the discriminant condition (which must be negative for no real solutions):
$$(-4)^2 - 4(2)(-t) < 0$$
$$16 + 8t < 0$$
$$t < -2$$

steps.title: Identify which choice satisfies the condition
steps.content:
We need $t < -2$. Checking each choice:
  • A) $-3 < -2$ ✓
  • B) $-1 < -2$ ✗
  • C) \$1 < -2$ ✗
  • D) \$3 < -2$ ✗

That matches choice A — A is correct.
